jobs_results.title: Healthcare Executive Recruiter
jobs_results.description:
Healthcare Executive Recruiter

CoreMedical Group is looking for a high energy, driven, team member who is ready to work hard and play hard in our Manchester, New Hampshire office! We’re looking for individuals who want to make a difference in the lives of healthcare professionals across the country in a sales-based recruiter role...

As a Healthcare Recruiter you’ll help people find their dream jobs – while you come to work every day at yours!

Here are some of our benefits and perks
• Uncapped Commissions
• 4.4 out of 5 stars on Glassdoor (113 reviews) with 94% approval of CEO!
• Named "Best Company to Work For" TWICE (BusinessNH Magazine)
• Named "Coolest Company to Work For" by stayworkplay.org
• Newly constructed office, fitness facility, game room, start-up culture
• Full healthcare benefits, access to many ancillary plans as well as matching 401k
• We pay for a Financial Advisor to give you investment and retirement advice

What You’ll Do As A Healthcare Executive Recruiter:
• Match nurses and healthcare professionals with new jobs across the country
• Coach candidates through the job search process
• Maintain strong relationships with your candidates to ensure they’re happy in their new jobs
• Achieve professional and personal growth by hitting sales and revenue goals
• Work with a fun, inspiring team of people in Manchester, NH

What We Look For In A Healthcare Executive Recruiter:
• Competitive, motivated personality
• Independent thinker who can make quick decisions
• Hard worker who will achieve sales growth over time
• Sales or Customer Service experience is a bonus

At Core You’ll Get:
• A fun, exciting office environment: Whether it’s the gong ringing for a new deal or a shuffleboard tournament in the game room, there’s never a dull moment!
• Intensive sales training for new employees: We set you up for success as a Sales Recruiter from day one – we’ve always got your back!
• Recognition for your hard work: Company-wide rallies, unlimited earning potential, and company-sponsored outings all show our appreciation for the hard work you put in every day.
• An opportunity to give back: We participate in ongoing charity projects to support our New Hampshire community.
• Health and wellness opportunities: If working out is your thing, take advantage of our on-site gym and basketball hoops or the hiking trails around the lake right outside our door.
• An all-inclusive, all expenses paid, vacation: Every employee has the chance to earn a spot on our annual Club CoreMed trip to an all-inclusive resort in the Caribbean.

Contact us today to meet the team, visit our office, and learn more about a Recruiter career at CoreMedical Group!

CoreMedical Group provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability (physical/mental), genetic information, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

This policy applies to all terms and conditions of

employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination,

layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training

jobs_results.title: Recruiting Coordinator
jobs_results.description:
Recruiting Coordinator Needed!

BrettonTrova is a southern NH-based permanent recruiting agency focused on providing high-quality services to nurses and other healthcare professionals seeking jobs throughout New England. We are growing rapidly and looking to hire the right professional for our nurse recruiting team...

At BrettonTrova it is our mission to improve the job-seeking experience for employees and employers alike. We strive to impact the communities we serve by engaging and linking exceptional healthcare workers with their ideal job.

Position Details:
• Full time, Monday - Friday
• $40K - $50K
• Candidate sourcing
• Conduct Texting and email campaigns
• Database management
• Resume formatting and other administrative tasks as assigned
• Contribute to, and be responsible to daily, weekly, and monthly individual and team goals

Qualifications:
• Microsoft Office experience
• Motivated, positive individual and able to work in a fast-paced team environment a must
• Organized and proficient with both written and verbal communication
• New Grads are eligible for this position

Why Join BrettonTrova?
• An opportunity to join one of the fastest growing health care staffing companies in the industry
• A leadership team that will get in the trenches, develop you, care about you and support your professional and personal goals
• Uncapped income and uncapped career growth opportunities
• A culture and a collective group of people that is second to none.
• A team. No us vs them, no finger pointing, no placing blame. A group that is all rowing in the same direction trying to do what’s best for our providers and clients.
• A winning culture and a fun place to work

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $40,000.00 - $50,000.00 per year

Benefits:
• 401(k)
• 401(k) matching
• Health insurance
• Life insurance
• Paid time off
• Professional development assistance
• Referral program
• Vision insurance

Experience level:
• No experience needed

Schedule:
• Monday to Friday

Ability to commute/relocate:
• Manchester, NH 03101: Reliably commute or planning to relocate before starting work (Required)

Experience:
• Recruiting: 1 year (Preferred)

Work Location: One location

jobs_results.title: Recruiter
jobs_results.description:
Come join our team at Greater Seacoast Community Health! We are seeking mission driven, dedicated individuals who want to make a difference in their community.

Greater Seacoast Community Health is a network of community health centers providing primary care, pediatrics, dental care, prenatal care, behavioral health counseling, substance abuse disorder treatment, mobile health services, WIC... social work services, a pharmacy, parenting classes, playgroups and home visiting.

Join our growing Human Resources Department as the Recruiter where you will recruit for all our positions: medical, dental, WIC, social work, behavioral health, accounting, billing, information technology, marketing, development, Strafford County Public Health Network, SOS Recovery Community Organization, and all others.

Typically works four (4) days per week in our Somersworth, NH office (Goodwin Community Health) and one (1) day per week at our office in Portsmouth, NH (Families First Health Center).

Job Duties:
• Sources candidates for all positions in the health center, to include; clinical and non-clinical positions, support positions through senior leadership/c-suite roles, and clinical providers
• Review all employment applications using our Web-based applicant tracking system, ADP Workforce Now
• Determine which candidates move forward in the hiring process
• Schedule interviews for hiring managers with candidates and solicit feedback from hiring managers using our online form
• Create offer letters
• Ensure that candidates receive appropriate immunizations though our external vendor
• Collect and verify required employment documents and professional credentials
• Coordinate and help conduct first-day new-hire orientation meetings

Qualification:
• Bachelors Degree required
• 1-2 years of recruiting experience is required, healthcare recruiting preferred

Benefits:

Our comprehensive benefits package includes health, dental and vision insurance with an agency subsidy. One of our health plans has a no-premium option for employee-only coverage. We also offer a 401(k) plan with an employer match and employer-paid group term life insurance and short-term disability coverage. Voluntary benefits include long-term disability coverage, two Flexible Spending Accounts, and access to discounted auto and home insurance and pre-paid legal assistance. We offer earned time (vacation and sick leave) plus six paid holidays. Eligibility starts at 24 hours/week, with full benefits offered at 32 hours/week and above.

About Us

Greater Seacoast Community Health is a network of non-profit community health centers providing primary care, pediatrics, dental care, prenatal care, behavioral health counseling, substance use disorder treatment, mobile health services, WIC, social work services, a pharmacy, parenting classes, playgroups and home visiting. Services are open to everyone and aim to be respectful, recovery-friendly, LGBTQ-affirming and trauma-informed. We are recognized as a Patient-Centered Medical Home and emphasize a team-based approach to care. Learn more at GetCommunityHealth.org.

Greater Seacoast Community Health is an Equal Opportunity provider of health care and other services and an Equal Opportunity employer

jobs_results.title: Healthcare Recruiter
jobs_results.description:
Healthcare Recruiter

Full Time (40hrs Per Week...

We are looking for a dynamic, detail oriented healthcare recruiter to join our corporate team. Main duties:
• Demonstrate outstanding planning, organization, prioritization, calendar management, and a sense of urgency to staff open positions.
• Achieve production objective established by management by making a high volume of cold calls; take a “hands on” approach to recruiting; not rely on job boards or job postings as a means of recruiting
• Recruit, identify, select and prequalifying candidates relative to listings
• Partner closely and work collaboratively with network facilities to fulfill open positions
• Assist with screening resumes and pre-qualifies candidates for openings via website, phone or interview.
• Collect data from network facilities to create weekly analytic reports for Executive staff
• Maintain established relationships with local nursing schools to recruit new grad nursing students

Must have computer proficiency, especially with excel and Microsoft Office. Have strong organizational skills and a strong attention to detail.

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $65,000.00 - $75,000.00 per year

Benefits:
• 401(k)
• Dental insurance
• Health insurance
• Vision insurance

Schedule:
• 8 hour shift

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Haverhill, MA 01832

Implement recruitment strategies to identify highly qualified candidates for active job openings. Create and post new listings on various job boards to attract applicants. Screen resumes, cover letters, and application materials for qualifications and experience. Conduct phone, video, or in-person interviews to assess a candidate's fit. Check references to verify a candidate's work history... skills, and experience. Coordinate and schedule interviews with hiring managers or members of the nursing management team.

Bachelors degree in Human Resources, Nursing, or Equivalent preferred. Previous experience as a Recruiter, Preferred. Licensed Registered Nurse or Licensed Practical Nurse, Preferred

jobs_results.title: Travel Allied Health Professional - CT Technologist
jobs_results.description:
Atlas MedStaff is seeking a travel CT Technologist for a travel job in Haverhill, New Hampshire.

Job Description & Requirements...
• Specialty: CT Technologist
• Discipline: Allied Health Professional
• Start Date: 03/11/2024
• Duration: 13 weeks
• 40 hours per week
• Shift: 8 hours, flexible
• Employment Type: Travel

Technologist, Computed Tomography

City: Haverhill, NH

Atlas Medstaff is currently seeking candidates in the Technologist (Computed Tomography) profession for a 13 week contract in the Haverhill, NH area. Atlas Medstaff is a premier staffing firm dedicated to matching outstanding talent with exceptional opportunities. Our commitment to excellence and our consultative approach has helped us become a premier leader in the staffing industry.

Atlas MedStaff offers a rich benefits package that includes: Comprehensive Health, Dental and Vision insurance. 401k with matching after 6 months. Telehealth services for ease of use when an office visit isn’t feasible. Voluntary Term Life and Dependent Life coverage. Voluntary Term Life and Dependent Life coverage as well as In Hospital Cash Plans by Ternian. All of this and more available through a unique online portal that gives you the flexibility on the road to make changes as you see fit.

Atlas MedStaff Job ID #1125250. Pay package is based on 8 hour shifts and 40 hours per week (subject to confirmation) with tax-free stipend amount to be determined. Posted job title: CT Tech

About Atlas MedStaff

We think your recruiter and your travel healthcare agency should care about you. Our team has lots of crazy ideas like that. Like co-founders who are transparent, a tight-knit community for our travelers, creating content that tells *your* story, and helping you find adventure.

Every Atlas traveler gets an Atlas Adventure™, an adventure that is uniquely *you*. Skiing, mountain climbing, skydiving, or even just a weekend at a spa or tickets to a concert you can’t wait for.

We have been ranked #1 by BluePipes as the best travel healthcare agency. And received the distinction of being the highest-rated travel healthcare staffing agency by Great Recruiters. Both of those recognitions are fueled by real reviews by real travel nurses and allied healthcare professionals that work with our team.

Atlas has been recognized as one of the fastest-growing companies in the country by INC; the fastest-growing staffing company in the country, and the fastest-growing healthcare staffing company in the country by Staffing Industry Analysts; and we recently received the AMN Perfect Partner Award. Many of these recognitions we have won multiple times.

Awards are great, but it's our travelers — our people — that we are most proud of. And every Atlas traveler is part of our team. You may not be in the office (heck, we’re not either since a lot of our team has been working remote since the pandemic), but you are part of the Atlas Community.

If you don’t have this kind of relationship with your current recruiter and travel nurse agency, maybe it’s time you try one that’s a little bit different.

We’re here for you.

What will your Atlas Adventure™ be?

Benefits
• Medical benefits
• 401k retirement plan
• Vision benefits
• Dental benefits

jobs_results.title: Recruiter
jobs_results.description:
At MAS Medical Staffing, our Home Care team provides services to allow seniors to remain in their home and meet the challenges aging with dignity, care, and compassion.

About the Role ...

The Home Care Recruiter is responsible for acquiring new talent to the Company by managing the full recruiting lifecycle.

Supporting the Home Care department as its customer, this role will oversee multiple concurrent talent searches to meet the business needs of the Home Care department while providing an exceptional candidate experience that represents the values of MAS Medical Staffing.

As the Home Care Recruiter, you will :
• Partner with hiring leaders to understand hiring needs and to effectively develop and execute recruitment strategies.
• Effectively pre-screen / interview candidates to determine skills and fit in relation to role requirements; serve as the primary point of contact throughout the entire recruitment process.
• Initiate and manage onboarding / new hire process to ensure a positive transition from candidate to new employee.
• Manage full recruiting lifecycle and process from intake discussion through offer acceptance; provide value-added guidance on all aspects of the hiring process to ensure the right talent is selected.
• Meet department goals and metrics related to calls, screens / interviews, and hires.
• Accurately manage and maintain candidate profiles and onboarding process in Bullhorn.
• Always maintain appropriate confidentiality.
• Partner with Talent Acquisition team members on new business recruiting
• Identify and implement process improvement opportunities to ensure an effective and efficient process that minimizes waste and optimizes the hiring of top talent.
• Attending career fairs and other on-site recruiting events
• Perform other duties as assigned.

In this position, you must have :
• Strong internal motivation to get the job done, with a high bar for excellence.
• High degree of professionalism and collaboration
• Independent / flexible problem-solver with the ability to adapt as the business grows
• Display accountability, always meeting deadlines and keeping commitments
• Comfortable working in a fast-paced team with changing priorities

Education / Experience / Licensure :
• High school diploma required; bachelor' s degree in related field preferred
• Minimum of 2 years of professional recruiting experience, including high-volume recruiting
• One year of Call Center or Customer Service experience
• Excellent interpersonal and communication skills
• Excellent customer service skills and phone etiquette
• Proficient in MS Office
• Experience using virtual platform for interviewing
• Experience with Healthcare recruiting preferred
• Experience In Human Resources and Talent Acquisition preferred

Location :

The Home Care Recruiter is a hybrid position located in Manchester, NH with the expectation of being in the office a minimum of two days per week.

Why You ll Love Working at MAS :

The MAS Home Care team is a collection of high performing and highly engaged team members. We re proud of our culture, benefits, and commitment to work-life balance, including :
• Excellent employee benefits, including health & dental insurance, 401k with company match, and unlimited Paid Time Off
• A modern, brick and timber loft headquarters office in Manchester, New Hampshire
• Opportunities to engage with fellow MAS employees through Culture, Service, and Learning & Development committees.
• The opportunity to work for a growing company in a critical and fast-paced sector within healthcare. MASinternal

Last updated : 2024-01-26

options.text: Epic Travel Staffing (formerly Emerald Health Services)
